title: Ensure Block Storage is encrypted with KYOK

input_row.title: Ensure Block Storage is encrypted with KYOK
input_row.description: By default, IBM Cloud Block Storage provides provider-managed encryption for all data. For enhanced security, customers can bring their own encryption keys and manage them through IBM Key Management Service – Hyper Protect Crypto Services (HPCS). The customer can choose to use BYOK instead of provider-managed keys for enhanced security
input_row.rationale: When it comes to encryption of data at rest in IBM Cloud, the best practice is to allow customers use encryption capabilities offered by Hardware Security Module crypto service. IBM Cloud Hyper protect Crypto Service offers Keep your Own Key (KYOK) capabilities that allow customers to have exclusive key control over encryption keys. This provides a significant level of control over encryption keys where only authorized users have access (no privileged users included IBM Cloud Admins) to encryption keys, in turn increases both security control as well as meet relevant compliance requirements.
input_row.audit: You can use the IBM Cloud Block Storage configuration properties to verify whether a Cloud Object Storage bucket is enabled to use Hyper Protect Crypto Service. From Console, Navigate to your Cloud Object Storage instance: 1. In the IBM Cloud console, go to Menu icon > VPC Infrastructure > Storage > Block storage volumes. A list of all block storage volumes displays. 2. For each of the volumes listed, ensure the encryption field pointing to "Hyper Protect".
input_row.remediation: You will not be able to add Hyper Protect Crypto Services as the key management service once data is already written to a Cloud Block Storage Volumes. In order to ensure that objects are encrypted using Hyper Protect Crypto Services you will need to create a new Cloud Block Storage volume, set it to use Hyper Protect Crypto Services key management service and then upload/copy the existing objects to this new volume.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control can be fully verified using IBM Cloud APIs, specifically regarding the configuration of IBM Cloud Block Storage, which includes properties like encryption settings.
- The audit field provides a clear approach to check the encryption status of the block storage volumes, which indicates it is a standard property within IBM Cloud's API capabilities.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_block_storage_encryption_with_hyper_protect_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Full verification can be achieved through the following IBM Cloud API calls:
  - Use the `ibm_cloud_block_storage.list()` method to enumerate all block storage volumes in the account.
  - For each block storage volume, check the `encryption` property to verify whether it is set to "Hyper Protect".
  
- Commands to be utilized from IBM Cloud CLI:
  - Run `ibmcloud is volume-list` to list all block storage volumes along with their properties.
  - Evaluate the output for the `encryption` field, looking specifically for the value "Hyper Protect".

- Specific resources to enumerate:
  - Block storage volumes within the selected account and region.

- Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:
  - The `encryption` property of each block storage volume should equal "Hyper Protect".

- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** If the encryption field for any block storage volume is set to "Hyper Protect".
  - **Fail:** If the encryption field for any block storage volume is set to "Provider managed" or is empty.

By implementing this automated approach, organizations can ensure they maintain compliance with the encryption standards suggested by the CIS benchmark for IBM Cloud.
